title: Approvals Feature from Discovery Notes
prompt:
Work only from the material below. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue.

We interviewed eight operations managers about a new approvals workflow for our SaaS product. Turn this into a reviewed requirements set for the first release.

Discovery notes:
- Managers need every purchase request above $5,000 to require manager approval.
- Finance wants optional second-stage approval above $20,000.
- Employees complain that current approvals are buried in email and get lost.
- Managers need a mobile-friendly way to approve or reject quickly.
- Finance requires a searchable audit trail of who approved, rejected, or delegated a request.
- Some companies want department-specific approval thresholds.
- Users need clear status visibility: pending, approved, rejected, sent back.
- Customers asked for comments on rejection so employees know what to fix.
- Non-functional expectations mentioned in interviews: approval actions should feel near-instant, the audit trail must be retained for at least 1 year, and only authorized approvers should act on a request.

Please produce requirements for first release, including priorities, non-goals, traceability to the discovery notes, and measurable non-functional requirements.

assertions.name: structured-requirements-artifact
assertions.description: Produces a structured requirements artifact rather than loose brainstorming notes

assertions.name: separates-functional-and-nfr
assertions.description: Separates functional requirements from non-functional requirements

assertions.name: includes-priorities
assertions.description: Assigns priorities such as P0/P1/P2 or equivalent

assertions.name: includes-traceability
assertions.description: Links requirements back to source interview findings

assertions.name: defines-non-goals
assertions.description: Calls out at least one clear non-goal or out-of-scope item

title: Chinese Expense Workflow Requirements
prompt:
只根据下面给出的材料工作，不要反问。如果需要假设，请简短说明后继续。

下面是老板、销售负责人、财务和法务给我的零散输入，请把它整理成一个第一版 requirements set，用于后续架构设计，不要直接进入 API 或数据库设计。

输入材料：
- 老板：移动端报销必须能拍照上传发票，不然一线销售不会用。
- 销售负责人：报销提交流程不能超过 3 分钟，否则大家会拖着不报。
- 财务：单笔超过 3000 元必须补充业务说明；超过 10000 元必须二级审批。
- 财务：我们需要看到谁在什么时候提交、审批、驳回，日志至少保留 2 年。
- 法务：涉及个人银行卡和身份证信息时，只有授权角色可以查看。
- 财务：月末高峰期系统也不能卡，至少要支持 500 个并发提交。
- 销售负责人：被驳回时必须告诉员工原因，不然他们会反复问。
- 老板：第一期先做国内员工，不做海外实体和多币种。
- 财务：需要导出月度报销汇总报表，但可以放到第二优先级。

请输出：功能需求、非功能需求、优先级、来源追踪、明确的不做项。

assertions.name: clear-requirement-statements
assertions.description: Outputs explicit requirement statements instead of repeating stakeholder notes

assertions.name: captures-priority-and-scope
assertions.description: Captures priorities and explicit out-of-scope items

assertions.name: captures-security-and-audit
assertions.description: Includes security, audit retention, and authorization requirements

assertions.name: quantifies-non-functional-requirements
assertions.description: Keeps non-functional requirements measurable, not vague

assertions.name: stays-in-requirements-layer
assertions.description: Does not primarily jump into implementation or schema design

title: Compliance-Sensitive Audit Log Requirements
prompt:
Work only from the material below. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue.

We are building an audit-log capability for a B2B finance platform. Please turn the notes below into a first-release requirements set before architecture starts.

Stakeholder notes:
- Compliance: audit records for financial actions must be retained for 7 years.
- Security: only explicitly authorized roles may search or export audit logs.
- Operations: support engineers need fast search by account, actor, date range, and action type.
- Product: customers want to export filtered audit logs as CSV.
- Compliance: exported data must be traceable to who exported it and when.
- Security: audit records themselves must not be editable or deletable through the product.
- Platform: month-end load is heavy; search must remain responsive.
- Legal: some regions require data access to be justifiable and reviewable.

Please output functional requirements, non-functional requirements, priorities, source traceability, and explicit non-goals. Stay in the requirements layer and do not jump into storage design or schema design.

assertions.name: captures-compliance-retention
assertions.description: Defines measurable retention requirements grounded in the compliance notes

assertions.name: captures-access-control-and-export-auditability
assertions.description: Includes authorization and export traceability requirements

assertions.name: captures-searchability-and-performance
assertions.description: Defines searchable dimensions and measurable responsiveness expectations

assertions.name: defines-immutability-and-non-goals
assertions.description: Includes audit-record immutability and at least one explicit non-goal

assertions.name: stays-in-requirements-layer
assertions.description: Does not primarily jump into storage, schema, or implementation design
